statement: A simple planar truss has 5 members and 4 joints. Joint A is pinned to the wall; joint D is on a roller. A 2000 N downward load acts at joint C. Find all member forces using the method of joints.
common_student_errors: Applying single-body equilibrium equations to the whole truss as if it were a rigid body with internal forces visible
Confusing the external reactions (solvable by statics) with the member forces (requires method of joints or sections)
Not recognizing this is a truss (multi-member connected body) and is out of scope for single rigid body statics
